free_text: Studies of alpha particle groups from the 31P(d,a)29Si
reaction in the deuteron energy range between 1.5 and
2.5 MeV.
Two independently rotating (from 20 to 160
degr.) semiconductor counters mounted in the horizontal
plane, one of them being used as a monitor.
Alpha particles emergent from the reaction were
identified by measuring their energy at various angles
and comparing the energy values obtained with those
calculated from the reaction kinematics.
Three or four investigated targets were placed
simultaneously on the target holder. The target was
Zn2P3 evaporated onto a thin silver backing. The
thickness of the backing reached 20 keV for the
deuteron energy of 2 MeV.
